argument: While the claim of holding simultaneous full-time roles as CEO of DataLoom and Infrastructure Engineer at Stripe may seem impractical, there are explanations consistent with the record that mitigate this discrepancy. The core of the claim is Devin Marsh’s involvement with DataLoom, which he founded in March 2019 [deck]. It's plausible that Marsh's role at DataLoom started part-time, evolving to full-time post-acquisition of additional responsibilities or after leaving Stripe, aligning with typical startup founder trajectories. Additionally, the claim is self-reported in a venue (the seed deck) that might contain aspirational or simplified narratives. Verification notes highlight that the claim does not negate his role at Stripe, but rather overlaps are often found minimized in personal profiles as 'full-time' does not always reflect actual hour counts or weekly structure proportionate to temporal contexts across tech startups.
